items.title: Integrity, Intelligence, and Energy
items.summary: And another thing. Didn't you just muse? I'm on a roll…
items.content_html:
And another thing.

Didn't you just muse?

I'm on a roll here.

Repeat after me:

Rejection is not the horrible thing.

Rejection is not the horrible thing.

What is the horrible thing?

What is the horrible thing?

Stop.

Choosing, or being chosen by, the horrible thing.

There is blessing in rejection.

In failure.

In a lot of near misses.

Reframe your rejections.

Reevaluate your failures.

Sometimes the worst thing is the "Yes."

To unanswered prayers.

But by the grace of God...

You might be dodging bullets.

Because sometimes, a lot of times, it's the hits that end of being fatal.

You want to know the greatest curse that looks like a blessing?

May your every wish come true.

On that happy note.

Muser out.

items.title: Not For Everyone
items.summary: How many people "get" you? You know what I'm asking here?…
items.content_html:
How many people "get" you?

You know what I'm asking here?

I think that's what you're looking for.

If you're wondering.

You need to find your people.

There are two types of people.

Maybe more.

There are those who just stare at you when you are sharing a musing.

And then there are those who are on the floor.

Rolling.

At its punchline.

I tend to like the latter more.

I mean, don't be fake, but I like the belly bwahahaers.

My favorite compliments are those who complain their stomaches hurt from laughing so much.

It just tickles me elbow.

I can tell a fake laugh from a real one a country mile away.

So don't be throwing any courtesy giggles over here.

I don't want any.

But I'm taking notes.

The people that get "it" and have "it."

That's who you want to be.

The people that "get" you.

That's who you want to find.

items.title: Big Bang Shot
items.summary: You still talking about moonshots? What's that now? To the moon!…
items.content_html:
You still talking about moonshots?

What's that now?

To the moon!

Like you Ralph Kramden.

The better term is Marsshot.

Former owner of the Reds?

Ok, Marsshot looks terrible.

Double s's.

But I think that's what they'll call it.

No longer aiming for "just" the moon.

Going further.

Farther.

Me?

I'm gunning for Alpha Centauri.

Seems doable.

AC Starfaring.

No use, never use, in playing short.

A trillion is a million millions.

Just thought I'd share that.

🚀

items.title: AlexX
items.summary: SpaceX goes public tomorrow. If you're into that sort of thing.
items.content_html:
SpaceX goes public tomorrow.

If you're into that sort of thing.

Going public and sheet.

As a private eye guy, I will be investigating.

When it occurs to me.

Sometime mid-afternoon.

Early evening.

You'd think SpaceX was all about space.

And as a Starfaring fellow, that I'd be all in.

But it's not.

And I'm not.

It's mostly about space.

Rockets.

Which generates money.

And satellites.

Which makes money.

To turn earth into a Dyson Sphere.

But it's also about ai.

Which is costing them a fortune.

The ai has to help build out Mars.

But all I know is ai in general will win, I just don't know who that means.

Like automobiles and car companies.

Internet and software.

Of course, fortunes are often relative.

Levels, man, levels...

The fortune lost thus far should be made up for (exponentially) tomorrow.

People are all over the place on this IPO.

Salvation or ruin.

Boom or bust.

Both?

Probably both.

Polarization at its finest.

I got my Coke Zero ready.

Cheers.

Almost game time.

Time for war paint.

If you're into that.

🎲

items.title: Too Good To Be True
items.summary: It occurs to me, that a lot of my stories are…
items.content_html:
It occurs to me, that a lot of my stories are old.

They are old.

Like you.

I'm not old, though.

I'm oldish.

This is done intentionally.

With intention.

Most of today's stories go like this.

I was with my lovely daughter the other day.

We were hanging out.

As we are wont to do.

When she asked, "How is your day going?"

"It is going wonderful. How is yours?"

"It's great!"

"Good."

"Do you want to know why I asked how your day is going?"

"Of course."

"Because I wanted to start a conversation with you."

"Well, it worked. What should we talk about next?"

And off we went.

These are my glory days.

My favorite stories days.

They are so good, I sometimes keep them hidden.

It sometimes feels like a dream.

So for now, I'll mostly share old stories.

New musings.

Old stories.

And sometimes, only once in a while, tell you about my day.

items.title: We Could Move to South Oregon
items.summary: Do I tell more stories? Or do I tell more musings?…
items.content_html:
Do I tell more stories?

Or do I tell more musings?

Probably both.

Probably both.

When I was working in Menlo Park, in the heart of venture financing, I was working for a law partner who advised, "You need to listen to your inner voice. What's it saying to you, right now?"

And I heard it.

My inner voice.

It said one word.

One word only.

Calmly.

Definitively.

"Run."

Now trust me, no one missed me leaving there.

And I hate vehemently dislike running.

But I thank the partner.

And your inner voice.

Mmhmm.

Off I went.

I had achieved a dream out there in Silicon Valley.

But it wasn't mine.

It was never mine.

And in a way that I am, as Koreans are, I honored my family by becoming a lawyer.

And then moved across the country to do it.

The former being the Korean part of me.

The latter being the me part.

But if I'm honest, I always wanted to live in California.

And I never knew what I wanted to be when I grew up.

So law school in Cali felt like an answer.

Or at least, a three year delay in having to find one.

This is, in hindsight, terrible reasoning.

But I think terrible reasoning is a foundational component of youth.

Of middle and old age, too.

All of that to say, as I always say, everything - and I mean everything - leads me to right here.

Right now.

Living out in SV reinforced my love of technology and possibility.

Leaving it reinforced my love of a simple life.

Besides, with these little slabs of silicon, you can do anything anywhere in the Actuverse.

Is he making up words?

To chasing dreams.

Finding them.

And leaving them by the ocean.

Hallelujah.

items.title: Nothing More Expensive
items.summary: Bobby Lee tells this story. Who's that? Bruce's cousin? Bobby is…
items.content_html:
Bobby Lee tells this story.

Who's that? Bruce's cousin?

Bobby is a standup comedian and podcaster.

He tells this story about driving around in his Hyundai when he notices the low fuel light blinking.

Naturally, he stops to fill up at the nearest gas station.

And as he's pulling up, he decides to reward himself.

With an energy drink.

So he goes in the station and grabs the drink and heads to the counter.

There, a young Eastern European lady is standing alone, working.

She looks miserable.

Shackled.

"Hello, I just want this drink, please."

She takes his drink and starts to ring it up. 

She then looks at him, smirks and asks, "What do you do for a living?"

Bobby responds, "I'm a podcaster."

"I don't know what that means. Is that radio?"

"Oh, no, it's online."

"Oh."

A few seconds go by, and Bobby's curiosity gets the best of him:

"Why do you ask?"

The cashier looks up, pauses, and says,

"You look free."

Bobby explains a weight lifted off his shoulders and he just started to weep a little.

I don't know if you can take a comedian's stories.

I know you can't take their jokes.

But I think, I think, stories are fair game.

Besides, I'm not saying it happened to me.

I'm just passing it along.

Because I don't think there are many compliments finer than that.

🆓

items.title: Meta Mile
items.summary: You know how rappers have diss tracks? Where they battle their…
items.content_html:
You know how rappers have diss tracks?

Where they battle their fellow rapper foes via lyrical warfare?

I think we should do that at the corporate levels.

Diss tracks between F500 company CEOs.

I wouldn't buy 'em.

But I'd listen.

It doesn't even have to be rival C-Level quarrels.

It can be internal.

You gotta problem with Fred in HR?

Diss track.

He gotta problem with you?

Response track.

Loser leaves the company.

It ain't festivus.

But there is an airing of grievances.

Now, I realize this puts an awfully skewed amount of value towards rapping ability (likely not at all related to your occupation), but hey I don't make the rules.

I think corporate rap battles is the one thing missing from this silly world.

Of course there are (many, many) others, but I like to keep things simple.

But would you be shocked?

At all?

Even a little?

It kind of fits.

If you see Marky Zee on stage looking angry in a gi, ballcap turned to the side, and a mic in his hand.

With a beat on behind him that you don't wanna like, but kinda do.

You kinda do.

Well, what I can say except:

You're welcome.

items.title: Big and Small
items.summary: Quantum is one of my favorite words. I got a lot…
items.content_html:
Quantum is one of my favorite words.

I got a lot of them.

Actualization.

Starfaring.

Iterating.

But quantum's up there.

You want to know why?

Because it means two very different things at the same time.

It means opposite things simultaneously.

Quantum can be defined as something so small it's practically zero.

The notch right above nothing.

The smallest of the smallest.

At the same time, quantum can also refer to something very big.

Huge.

Significant.

This is not even to mention its use in physics, mechanics, computing.

It's the word de jour, a polarizing one to be sure (my favorites usually are), but it'll likely be an important word from here out.

Everything is measured.

Now more than ever.

May your life be quantum.

For the future, like the past, and the present, is.

0️⃣ and ♾

items.title: Songs of the Desert
items.summary: You know what's a good song? It's a poem, really. Like…
items.content_html:
You know what's a good song?

It's a poem, really.

Like all the best songs are.

Poetry.

I Hope You Dance.

I hope you never lose your sense of wonder.

I won't quote every line.

You get your fill to eat but always keep that hunger.

But I want to.

May you never take one single breath for granted.

Deep breath.

So good.

The songwriter wrote it by the ocean.

I hope you still feel small when you stand beside the ocean.

Which is where that line comes from.

🌊 .

Whenever one door closes, I hope one more opens.

🚪

Or two.

Three would be nice.

Promise me that you'll give faith a fighting chance.

Promise me.

A fighting chance.

I promise.

Be good, ya'll.

You know where I'll be.

💃🕺
